name: @abtree/improve-tree

steps.instruct: If $LOCAL.session_ref is set, use it. Otherwise pick the most recent non-current execution from `abtree execution list` and write its id to $LOCAL.session_ref. If $LOCAL.tree_path is not already set, ask the user for the absolute or repo-relative path to the source tree file (`.json`/ `.yaml`/`.yml`) the session ran against and store it at $LOCAL.tree_path — abtree does not record the source path on the execution document, so the caller has to supply it. Read `.abtree/executions/<id>.json` and the parsed tree definition at `.abtree/snapshots/<execution.snapshot>.json` to extract evidence from the run: how far it got, which nodes failed, retry counts, $LOCAL keys that ended up null vs populated, any stage_halt or failure statuses. Store the evidence (a structured object summarising the run) at $LOCAL.session_evidence.

Score how effectively the tree drove the session. Consider: did the tree reach `done`, were any retries exhausted, were instructions clear enough that the agent never had to ad-lib, did evaluate gates fire on the values they should, were any $LOCAL keys set-but-unused or used-but-unset, did parallel composites order their children sensibly. Score in [0, 1] (1 = the tree drove the session cleanly with no friction). Per observation include: node_path, severity (low/med/high), evidence (one line pointing at what in $LOCAL.session_evidence justifies it). Store at $LOCAL.effectiveness_score = { score, observations }.

Read $GLOBAL.authoring_guide for the tree authoring conventions. Propose concrete improvements to the tree at $LOCAL.tree_path. Each improvement is an object: { kind: rename|reword|split|merge|add-evaluate|add-retries|other, target: node_name or yaml path, change: short description of the edit, rationale: what $LOCAL.session_evidence motivates it }. Don't propose work the codebase already does well. Store the list at $LOCAL.improvements.

Write a draft plan to plans/<YYYY-MM-DD>-improve-<basename of $LOCAL.tree_path>.md (strip the .json/.yaml/.yml extension from the basename). Frontmatter: id, title, status: draft, author (from git config user.name), created (today), reviewed_by (empty). Body sections: ## Summary — one paragraph naming the tree (refer to it by $LOCAL.tree_path), the session id, and the headline score. ## Effectiveness score — the score and the observations (each with node_path, severity, evidence). ## Improvements — the proposal list with kind/target/change/rationale. ## Open questions — anything that needs codeowner input before the changes are applied. Store the relative path at $LOCAL.plan_path.

Stage $LOCAL.plan_path, create a single commit with message "docs(plans): improve-tree review of $LOCAL.tree_path (session $LOCAL.session_ref)", then push to the current branch's upstream. Capture the new HEAD SHA into $LOCAL.commit_sha. If the working tree had unrelated modifications, stage only $LOCAL.plan_path — do not bundle them. If push fails (no upstream, network, etc.), submit failure with the error message rather than retrying.

description: Score the effectiveness of a tree using evidence from one of its sessions, find improvements in parallel, draft a plan in plans/, then commit and push.

global.authoring_guide: run `abtree docs author` for the YAML authoring guide and the rules a tree should satisfy
